flashcards.back: A recurrence relation is an equation that defines a sequence recursively, expressing the nth term as a function of previous terms, typically used to describe the time complexity of recursive algorithms.

flashcards.front: What is the Master Theorem used for?
flashcards.back: The Master Theorem provides asymptotic analysis for recurrence relations of the form T(n) = aT(n/b) + f(n), where a≥1, b>1, and determines time complexity for divide-and-conquer algorithms.

flashcards.front: What is the substitution method for solving recurrences?
flashcards.back: The substitution method involves guessing a solution form and then using mathematical induction to prove its correctness.
A recursion tree is a visual representation that breaks down a recursive algorithm into levels, showing the cost at each level, used to analyze total cost of recursive calls.

flashcards.front: Define T(n) for binary search recurrence
flashcards.back: T(n) = T(n/2) + O(1) - the problem size halves with each recursive call, with constant work at each level.

flashcards.front: What is the recurrence for Merge Sort?
flashcards.back: T(n) = 2T(n/2) + O(n) - divides problem into two equal halves, conquers recursively, then merges in linear time.

flashcards.front: What is iteration method in recurrence analysis?
flashcards.back: The iteration method expands a recurrence repeatedly until a pattern is visible, then solves the resulting summation.

flashcards.front: What does O(log n) mean for binary search?
flashcards.back: Binary search makes at most log₂(n) + 1 comparisons, halving the search space with each recursive call.

flashcards.front: What is space complexity of recursive algorithms?
flashcards.back: Space complexity includes stack space for recursive calls. For binary search: O(log n) stack space; for merge sort: O(n) auxiliary space.
If f(n) = O(n^(log_b a - ε)) where ε > 0, then T(n) = Θ(n^(log_b a)). The subproblems dominate the combine work.
If f(n) = Θ(n^(log_b a)), then T(n) = Θ(n^(log_b a) log n). The subproblems and combine work are equal.
If f(n) = Ω(n^(log_b a + ε)) with regularity condition, then T(n) = Θ(f(n)). The combine work dominates.
T(n) = T(n-1) + T(n-2) + O(1), with base cases T(0)=T(1)=1. Naive implementation gives exponential time O(2^n).
Recursive algorithms create call stacks; complexity depends on number of recursive calls, work per call, and how problem size reduces at each level.

flashcards.front: What is the recurrence for QuickSort?
flashcards.back: T(n) = T(k) + T(n-k-1) + O(n), where k is elements in one partition. Worst case: T(n)=T(n-1)+O(n), Best case: T(n)=2T(n/2)+O(n).
